**Summary:** Macroscopic entropy production σ^{(tot)} in the general nonlinear isothermal chemical reaction system with mass action kinetics is decomposed into a free energy dissipation and a house-keeping heat: σ^{(tot)}=σ^{(fd)}+σ^{(hk)}; σ^{(fd)}=-rd A/rd t, where A is a generalized free energy function. The search didn't return directly relevant amyloid kinetics papers, but the well-established structural principle is clear from the literature (Knowles/Meisl/Cohen master equation): aggregate mass `M(t)` grows via primary nucleation (monomer-driven source), elongation (linear in aggregate, seed-dependent), and secondary nucleation (autocatalytic, super-linear in aggregate and monomer-dependent), all bounded by a finite monomer pool (capacity term).

The best children (0.8885) embody this with a tunable-order capacity `(plateau-c)^(1+c8²)` and tunable autocatalytic order `c^(1+c5²)`. The shape loss is the dominant remaining cost (~0.38). 

My structural change for diversity + physical grounding: keep the autonomous, mass-conserving structure but make the **autocatalytic secondary-nucleation term also seed-dependent** through a multiplicative `(1+c·M0)` factor (seeded curves have a sharper early autocatalytic onset), and combine baseline + primary monomer-dependent source. This separates
...[6991 chars total]...
positive monomer feature `m0` and on `c ∈ [0, plateau] ≥ 0` with exponent `≥ 1`; capacity base is `≥ 0` with exponent `≥ 1`. No divisions, logs, or fractional/variable-sign powers — the RHS is real, finite, smooth, and vanishes at the plateau, so `odeint` and least-squares fitting stay well-conditioned. 10 fitted constants stays well under the 13 cap and remains reliably optimizable from one start.

The function remains straight-line with exactly one `evaluate_expression` call directly returned.
